content.text:
version=3.13
buildnr=
tag=
branch=
=== configure coq version check ===
12:#  published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 2.1 of   #
13:#  the License, or (at your option) any later version.                #
31:ignore_coq_version=false
32:ignore_ocaml_version=false
102:  -ignore-coq-version  Accept to use experimental or unsupported versions of Coq
103:  -ignore-ocaml-version Accept to use experimental or unsupported versions of OCaml
139:    -ignore-coq-version|--ignore-coq-version)
140:        ignore_coq_version=true;;
141:    -ignore-ocaml-version|--ignore-ocaml-version)
142:        ignore_ocaml_version=true;;
505:coq_ver=$(${COQBIN}coqc -v 2>/dev/null | tr -d '\r' | sed -n -e 's/The Coq Proof Assistant, version \([^ ]*\).*$/\1/p')
506:case "$coq_ver" in
507:  8.12.0|8.12.1|8.12.2|8.13.0|8.13.1|8.13.2|8.14.0|8.14.1|8.15.0|8.15.1|8.15.2|8.16.0|8.16.1)
508:        echo "version $coq_ver -- good!";;
510:        echo "version $coq_ver -- UNSUPPORTED"
511:        if $ignore_coq_version; then
512:            echo "Warning: this version of Coq is unsupported, proceed at your own risks."
514:            echo "Error: CompCert requires a version of Coq between 8.12.0 and 8.16.1"
519:        echo "Error: make sure Coq version 8.13.2 is installed."
524:ocaml_ver=`ocamlc -version 2>/dev/null | tr -d '\r'`
527:        echo "version $ocaml_ver -- good!";;
529:        echo "version $ocaml_ver -- UNSUPPORTED"
530:        if $ignore_ocaml_version; then
531:            echo "Warning: this version of OCaml is unsupported, proceed at your own risks."
533:            echo "Error: make sure OCaml version 4.05 to 4.14 is installed."
542:ocamlopt_ver=`ocamlopt -version 2>/dev/null | tr -d '\r'`
552:ocamlopt_opt_ver=`ocamlopt.opt -version 2>/dev/null | tr -d '\r'`
563:menhir_ver=`menhir --version 2>/dev/null | sed -n -e 's/^.*version \([0-9]*\).*$/\1/p'`
567:          echo "version $menhir_ver -- good!"
579:          echo "version $menhir_ver -- UNSUPPORTED"
580:          echo "Error: CompCert requires a version greater or equal to $MENHIR_REQUIRED."
585:        echo "Error: make sure Menhir version $MENHIR_REQUIRED or later is installed."
595:        echo "version $make_ver (command '$mk') -- good!"
602:  echo "Error: make sure GNU Make version 3.80 or later is installed."
607:  echo "One or several required tools are missing or too old.  Aborting."

content.text: apt's Coq is 8.18.0 but CompCert 3.13.1 requires Coq 8.12.0–8.16.1. I'll install the toolchain via apt (make/gcc/ocaml/menhir all compatible) and get a supported Coq 8.16.1 via opam.

content.text: opam and build tools installed. Now initialize opam with the system OCaml compiler and install a supported Coq (8.16.1) plus menhir.

input.command:
export OPAMROOT=/root/.opam OPAMYES=1
opam init --bare --disable-sandboxing --no-setup 2>&1 | tail -3
opam switch create compcert ocaml-system 2>&1 | tail -5
eval $(opam env --switch=compcert)
ocamlc -version; echo "switch ready"

content.text: Now install Coq 8.16.1 and menhir via opam (Coq builds from source — this takes a while). Running in background.

input.command:
export OPAMROOT=/root/.opam OPAMYES=1
eval $(opam env --switch=compcert)
opam install -y coq.8.16.1 menhir 2>&1 | tail -30
echo "INSTALL_EXIT ${PIPESTATUS[0]}"
coqc --version 2>&1 | head -2
menhir --version 2>&1 | head -1

content.text: cgroup memory is capped at 4 GiB — the parallel Coq build OOM-kills silently. I'll rebuild serially (jobs=1) to stay under the cap.

input.command:
export OPAMROOT=/root/.opam OPAMYES=1 OPAMJOBS=1
eval $(opam env --switch=compcert)
opam install -y --assume-depexts -j 1 coq.8.16.1 2>&1 | tail -6
echo "INSTALL_EXIT ${PIPESTATUS[0]}"
coqc --version 2>&1 | head -2

content.text: Stale `.cmi` from the old menhir. I'll clear the OCaml build artifacts (keeping the compiled `.vo` proofs) and rebuild.

input.command:
cd /tmp/CompCert
find . -name '*.cmi' -o -name '*.cmo' -o -name '*.cmx' -o -name '*.cmt' -o -name '*.cmti' -o -name '*.o' | grep -vE '/runtime/' | wc -l
find . \( -name '*.cmi' -o -name '*.cmo' -o -name '*.cmx' -o -name '*.cmt' -o -name '*.cmti' -o -name '*.o' \) -delete
# regenerate menhir-produced parser files
rm -f cparser/pre_parser.ml cparser/pre_parser.mli
echo "cleaned; vo count:"; find . -name '*.vo' | wc -l

content.text: The `find -delete` didn't execute (tool intercepts `find`). I'll remove stale OCaml artifacts with explicit globs.

input.command:
cd /tmp/CompCert
for d in cparser extraction driver lib common backend cfrontend x86 x86_64 MenhirLib flocq exportclight debug .; do
  rm -f "$d"/*.cmi "$d"/*.cmo "$d"/*.cmx "$d"/*.cmt "$d"/*.cmti "$d"/*.o 2>/dev/null
done
rm -f cparser/pre_parser.ml cparser/pre_parser.mli
echo "extraction cmi now: $(ls extraction/*.cmi 2>/dev/null | wc -l)"
echo "check vo present in lib: $(ls lib/*.vo 2>/dev/null | wc -l)"
echo "pre_parser.ml: $(ls cparser/pre_parser.ml 2>/dev/null || echo gone)"

content.text: `ccomp` built. Now verifying it's fully functional with an end-to-end compile-and-run test (standalone, without opam env in PATH).

input.command:
cd /tmp
cat > hello.c <<'EOF'
#include <stdio.h>
int fact(int n){ return n<=1?1:n*fact(n-1); }
int main(void){ printf("hello compcert %d\n", fact(5)); return 0; }
EOF
/tmp/CompCert/ccomp -v 2>&1 | head -1
echo "=== version ==="
/tmp/CompCert/ccomp --version 2>&1 | head -2
echo "=== compile & run ==="
/tmp/CompCert/ccomp -o /tmp/hello /tmp/hello.c 2>&1 && /tmp/hello; echo "run exit $?"

content.text:
CompCert is fully built and functional. Summary:

**Done.** CompCert C verified compiler (tag `v3.13.1`) is freshly built from source at `/tmp/CompCert/` and invokable via `/tmp/CompCert/ccomp`.

**What it took:**
- Environment: Ubuntu 24.04, `x86_64` → configured CompCert for **`x86_64-linux`** (`./configure x86_64-linux`).
- Toolchain: installed `build-essential` + `opam` via apt. CompCert 3.13.1 requires **Coq 8.12.0–8.16.1**, but apt only offers 8.18.0 (unsupported), so I built **Coq 8.16.1** in an opam switch on the system OCaml 4.14.1.
- Two build snags fixed: (1) the cgroup caps memory at **4 GiB**, which silently OOM-killed the parallel Coq compile — resolved by building serially (`-j1`); (2) the latest menhir (20260209) removed `MenhirLib.General` that CompCert 3.13.1 uses — pinned **menhir 20211230**, cleared stale OCaml artifacts, and rebuilt.
- Ran `make install` so the runtime `libcompcert.a` lands at the configured `stdlib_path`.

**Verification:** `ccomp --version` → "The CompCert C verified compiler, version 3.13"; compiling and running a recursive `fact(5)` C program via `/tmp/CompCert/ccomp` prints `hello compcert 120` and exits 0. (The lone `.note.GNU-stack` linker warning is a benign cosmetic notice from CompCert-generated assembly, not an error.)

result:
CompCert is fully built and functional. Summary:

**Done.** CompCert C verified compiler (tag `v3.13.1`) is freshly built from source at `/tmp/CompCert/` and invokable via `/tmp/CompCert/ccomp`.

**What it took:**
- Environment: Ubuntu 24.04, `x86_64` → configured CompCert for **`x86_64-linux`** (`./configure x86_64-linux`).
- Toolchain: installed `build-essential` + `opam` via apt. CompCert 3.13.1 requires **Coq 8.12.0–8.16.1**, but apt only offers 8.18.0 (unsupported), so I built **Coq 8.16.1** in an opam switch on the system OCaml 4.14.1.
- Two build snags fixed: (1) the cgroup caps memory at **4 GiB**, which silently OOM-killed the parallel Coq compile — resolved by building serially (`-j1`); (2) the latest menhir (20260209) removed `MenhirLib.General` that CompCert 3.13.1 uses — pinned **menhir 20211230**, cleared stale OCaml artifacts, and rebuilt.
- Ran `make install` so the runtime `libcompcert.a` lands at the configured `stdlib_path`.

**Verification:** `ccomp --version` → "The CompCert C verified compiler, version 3.13"; compiling and running a recursive `fact(5)` C program via `/tmp/CompCert/ccomp` prints `hello compcert 120` and exits 0. (The lone `.note.GNU-stack` linker warning is a benign cosmetic notice from CompCert-generated assembly, not an error.)
